title: ClientInner field order exploits Rust guaranteed drop order to keep comm_cfg alive during Cleanup
description: ClientInner declares fields in the order: client_ctx, comm_cfg, transport. Rust drops struct fields in declaration order (first declared = first dropped). The Drop impl for Client calls wh_Client_Cleanup (which internally uses comm_cfg pointers) before the implicit field drops run, so by the time transport is freed (which owns the memory that comm_cfg's pointers point into), wh_Client_Cleanup has already finished. The comment in the source makes this invariant explicit. A future contributor might reorder fields for aesthetic reasons or to group related types together, breaking the drop-order contract silently. The comment correctly documents this as a load-bearing ordering. An alternative would be ManuallyDrop on the transport field, but that would require unsafe boilerplate for a problem that field ordering already solves cleanly.
